text: The surgeon places standard ports for left-sided colonic or rectal surgery and establishes pneumoperitoneum. Adhesiolysis begins in the pelvis, with the bowel being dissected from the bladder wall. Adhesions between the sigmoid colon and cecum are separated, revealing the left and right colon mesentery. Dense adhesions are encountered in the liver area, where a tubular structure suspected to be the appendix is identified. Lateral dissection of the sigmoid colon is attempted due to dense adhesions, revealing the left gonad adhered to the sigmoid colon and a loop of redundant sigmoid colon adhered to the pelvis.

The surgeon identifies a virgin plane lateral to the appendix, allowing access below the fistula. The fistula is opened, exposing its epithelial surface, and the vaginal vault is carefully separated from the bowel.

The surgeon mobilizes the right colon and delivers the complex of adhered cecum and sigmoid colon through a mini laparotomy. The appendix is divided, and the cecum and sigmoid colon are separated. The medial approach is used to mobilize the sigmoid colon, with the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) divided and the splenic flexure taken down after dividing the inferior mesenteric vein (IMV).

The surgeon separates the omentum from the distal transverse colon and the greater curvature of the stomach, creating a tongue of omentum for patch repair. The fistula opening in the vaginal vault is demonstrated. A continuous suture repair is performed in two layers using V-Lock sutures, with the vaginal vault straightened using a swab on a stake during the repair.

The surgeon divides the mesorectum below the colonic opening of the fistula and transects the rectum using a stapler. The colon is delivered and transected through the previously created mini laparotomy. An omental patch repair is performed to separate the repaired vaginal opening from the anastomosis. The small bowel is repositioned inferior to the omental patch, which is placed along the right paracolic gutter.
The surgeon examines the integrity of the omental patch repair before proceeding with the anastomosis.
